title: vernonia liatroides subsp. ehrenbergiana
intro: Vernonia liatroides subsp. ehrenbergiana, also known as the Mexican Ironweed, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family that is native to the Americas. It possesses medicinal properties due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Vernonia liatroides subsp. ehrenbergiana include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
